title: TASK_PM: PS - Functionality Doc Testing & Content Integration
content:
TASK TITLE: PS - Functionality Doc Testing & Content Integration

-------------------------

Hey [PS Name]! 

We are ready to proceed with functionality doc testing and content integration for [Insert Staging Site Link]

RESOURCES: 

Here are the resources you'll need for testing:
- Access Info: [Link to Build Spec Server Details List]
- Current/Live Site Logins: 
- Functionality Doc: 
- GANTT Chart:
- TWFX Project: 
- Build Specs:
- Build Thread: 
- CI sheet:
- CI instruction list: [Link to 'Content Integration Instructions' checklist within Build Specs]
- Slickplan Sitemap:
- Prototypes: 
- Lead Designer: 

PART 1: FUNCTIONALITY DOC TESTING
1. Please follow this TO-DO list [Link to 'PS: Functionality Doc Testing' checklist within Main Project] and complete testing the blocks against the specs.
2. The goal of this testing is to spend ~ 1 hour each day testing the site against the functionality document to ensure that all of the intended functionality is present and works as expected. 
3. For any edits that you find, you can leave your comments in Project Huddle and manage the edits directly with the FED.

PART 2: CONTENT INTEGRATION 
1. Please follow this TO-DO list [Link to 'PS: Assigning Content Integration' checklist within Main Project] & make sure to kick off the CI to match up with the [insert date of Content Integration Begins milestone] date. Based on the Gantt Chart, we should aim to have CI complete by the end of the 2nd-to-last week of the build.

Please communicate here if you have any questions or concerns! 

Thank you.

title: Page Builder Explanation for Custom Redesigns
content:
Explanation for timeline/price for sites currently using page builders:

It looks like the site is using a page builder ([Builder Name]). A flag with sites using a builder is that clients may be expecting a lower cost. Some teams utilize page builders or pre-built themes as a means of quick development (cut corners for timeline, make up for a lack of technical development experience, etc.). All of our sites are custom-built with SEO, performance best practices, and scalability in mind. This ultimately is one of the big differentiators with our builds and pricing. If there are any concerns with pricing or timeline, we'd recommend a call to talk through this with the client!

title: Untitled
content:
Bill,

Thank you for sharing your feedback regarding the recent changes to time‑limited on‑street parking in Marymoor Village. Ensuring adequate and well‑managed parking resources is a priority for City leadership as Redmond’s urban centers continue to grow.

Meetings with the appropriate City staff, including representatives from the Planning Department and the Parking Enforcement program are reviewing concerns and discussing potential paths forward.

For context, the time limits in Marymoor Village were implemented to support short‑term parking for customers and visitors, helping maintain access to nearby businesses. Across Redmond’s urban centers, all time‑limited on‑street parking follows the same standard: free to use, with a 2‑hour limit between 9 a.m. and 9 p.m., Monday through Saturday. These changes are intended to encourage residents and employees to use off‑street parking or alternative transportation options, increasing availability of on‑street spaces for visitors. The updated approach is based on community feedback and the City’s Urban Centers Parking Study presented to the City Council in September 2025.

This strategy aligns with Redmond’s long‑term planning framework, including the Transportation Master Plan, which supports walkable, transit‑oriented urban centers and emphasizes managing limited curb space for the greatest community benefit.

We appreciate your email. Thank you.

title: Mythmoor Sailor Song
content:
November 11,2018
[11:09:52] <Freth> "Oh, sailin' we were on the east coast of Mythmoor."
[11:10:14] <Freth> "Lookin to pick up brick, 300 er more!"
[11:10:27] <Freth> "When all of a sudden me boat sprung a leak"
[11:10:50] <Freth> "Suddenly I'm thinkin' I'm up . creek"
[11:11:12] <Freth> "Sailin' ain't fer everyone, but it's fer me"
[11:11:30] <Freth> "Especially when ye leave and ye fergot ta pee!"
[11:11:51] <Freth> "So I'm holdin' me water, just like me ship."
[11:12:05] <Freth> "But we sailors always shoot from the hip."

title: Prompt groq
content:
Prompt de sistema

Eres un asistente experto en deportes que redacta emails personalizados y atractivos para aficionados. Tu tono es cercano, entusiasta y directo. Escribes siempre en español. El email debe tener: - Asunto sugerido en la primera línea con el formato: 
ASUNTO: [texto] 
- Saludo personalizado con el nombre del destinatario 
- 3-4 párrafos con noticias o contenido relevante sobre su deporte, basándote en la información que te proporcionan - Cierre motivador relacionado con el deporte 
- Firma: "El equipo de SportNews" Nunca inventes datos ni resultados. Usa solo la información proporcionada.

Prompt de usuario:
Genera un email para {{nombre}}, aficionado al {{deporte}}. 
Redacta el email completo con asunto incluido.
Aquí tienes la última noticia de {{deporte}} para inspirarte: 

{{contenido_rss}}
